No. l0461 /No. 46/RulesIDHC.-Inexercise ofthepo\.vers conferred by Section 7 of the Delhi High Court Act, 1966 (Act of 1966) read with Sections i22 anCl129 o/the Code ofCl'Vil Procedure, 1908and all other powers enabling it in this behalf, the High C our t of Delhi hereby makes.tile following ame~ldments in the Delhi High Court (Original side) Rules; 1967 :- 'i. ~n the existing rule 12 of the Chapter I , Delhi High Court (Original Side) Rules, 1967 u nder th e heading 1215 DG'l2002 ( 1 ) -- 1 of 3 -- 2 DELI-II GAZETTE: EXTRAORDINARY [PART IV " Office ho urs:;' t he words, "Any urgent ~iatter filed before 12.30 P.M. sh~ll be' put up. before the Court on the following w orking dai' shall stand substituted by the following words :- " Any urgent matter filed before 12 noon shall be put up before the Court for hearing on the following working day. T he following shall be substituted for the existing Cluause (i) of sub-rule (:""viii) (a) of Part B of Chapter 3 of .....~ -- 2 of 3 -- . PART IV] -/ DELHI GAZETTE: EXTRAORDINARY - 3 the High Court Rules and Orders, Volume V. pertaining to jurisdiction of Single Judge and of Benches of th e Court:- " (i) Petitions where vires of Acts or of statutory mles , regulations or bye-laws me challenged. ,- 2. The following Clauses shall be added after Clause (v) of sub-mle (wiii)(a) ofPmt B of Chapter 3 of the H ig h Court Rules and Orders, Volume V :- "(vi) Petitions pertaining to the award of Tenders. (vii) Petitions relating to Co-operative Societies. (viii) Petitions being service matters of Armed Forces of the Union . (i,--.:) Petitions arising out of Land Acquisitio n, (x) Petitions concerning orders passed by the High Court on the administrative side. " 3. The Explanation below. the above sub-mle (:\."v iii)(a) of Part B of Chapter 3 of the High Court rules and Orders , Volume V, shall consequently stand amended and substituted by the following :- "Explanation-The preliminary hearing for admission and final disposal of applications and petitions pertaining to matters mentioned in Clause (i) to (x) of sub-mle ( ;-"'v iii) (a) above shall how ever be before a Bench of two judges and before a Sing le Bench 'when there is no sitting of Di vision Ben ch. " 4. The existing third paragraph of Rule 1, PartA(b) of Chapter L High Court Rules and Orders, VoL V with the heading "Urgent petition to be presented personally" shall stand amended and 'substituted by th e following :-- ' "Appeals, petitions and applications etc. accompanied by a petition to treat the same as urgent, shaH be received at the Filing Counter only uptlJ 12 noon. In ex ceptional cases, the se ma y be received thereafter for hearing on the following day wi th the specific permission onIon 'bie the Chief Justice." These amen.dments shall come into force with immedi <-tt e e:ffect.
